title: ipomoea carnea subsp. fistulosa
intro: Ipomoea carnea subsp. fistulosa, also known as the "Mexican morning glory," is a type of flowering plant that belongs to the Convolvulaceae family. This plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and antimicrobial activities. The health benefits of Ipomoea carnea subsp. fistulosa include its ability to reduce fever, alleviate respiratory issues, and aid in the treatment of skin conditions such as eczema and acne. Traditionally, the plant is prepared as a tea or tincture, and its herbal preparations are also used topically to soothe skin irritations and wounds.
